task_completion.description: Verify the Tor connection status and exit node IP using check.torproject.org.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and navigated to https://check.torproject.org/ or https://check.torproject.org/api/ip, displaying a success message and an IPv4 address.

task_completion.description: Confirm connectivity to torproject.org and eff.org/pages/tor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser successfully loads https://www.torproject.org/ and https://www.eff.org/pages/tor (either in separate tabs or sequentially).

task_completion.description: Identify the browser's current Security Level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser's Security Level menu (usually accessed via a shield icon) or settings page is open, clearly showing the current level (Standard, Safer, or Safest).

task_completion.description: Draft the diagnostic report with all required exact uppercase headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the report being drafted. It MUST contain the exact headers: TOR CONNECTION DIAGNOSTIC REPORT, CONNECTION STATUS, EXIT NODE IP, SECURITY LEVEL, SITES TESTED, TIMESTAMP, and ANALYST NOTES.
A save dialog, text editor title bar, or terminal command confirms the file is saved exactly as 'tor_diagnostic_report.txt' inside the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a GUI web browser to check the sites and security level.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a graphical web browser (like Tor Browser or Firefox) being used to navigate the URLs and check the security level, rather than just using terminal commands like curl.

integrity.description: Verify the IP address and Security Level in the report match the actual observed values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The IPv4 address and Security Level typed into the text report must exactly match the values seen in the browser screenshots from check.torproject.org and the security settings.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually visited the sites before writing the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser navigation screenshots must appear chronologically before the final report is fully populated and saved.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The final report MUST be saved to the exact path: /home/ga/Documents/tor_diagnostic_report.txt. The report MUST contain the following exact uppercase section headers: 'TOR CONNECTION DIAGNOSTIC REPORT', 'CONNECTION STATUS', 'EXIT NODE IP', 'SECURITY LEVEL', 'SITES TESTED', 'TIMESTAMP', and 'ANALYST NOTES'.
